Top Staffing Trends Employers Must Watch in 2026 
The hiring landscape is evolving faster than ever — and companies that adapt early will win the talent race.
Here are the key staffing trends shaping 2026:
1. AI-Driven Hiring – Smarter screening, faster decisions, better matches
2. Skills Over Degrees – Companies prioritizing capability over credentials
3. Hybrid Workforce Models – Flexibility is now a competitive advantage
4. Speed-to-Hire Matters – Top talent won’t wait for slow processes
5. Contract & Flexible Staffing – Scalable hiring is the future
6. Employer Branding – Candidates choose companies, not just jobs
 The question is: Is your hiring strategy ready for 2026?
At Softwise Solutions, we help businesses stay ahead with future-ready staffing solutions.

I've written plenty about the good things that happen when marketing and sales teams adopt EverWorker. There's some bad things that happen too. Just not to our customers. 

What goes away? 

Vertical Agent Solutions, Legacy Vendors making a vaporware Agent washing play, and overpriced system providers with predatory "credit" based consumption and seat based offerings. 

For example: ZoomInfo, Semrush, Artisan and Outreach. Our customers have cancelled their contracts, or are planning on cancelling their contract soon as the term expires. We've eliminated the need on every level. 

The technographic, company news intel, and hiring signals  that ZI charges you an arm and a leg for? It's easily replaced with an AI Workflow that uses gpt 4.2 (yeah you don't need a powerful model) and some web search tools. Populates your CRM fields for cents with all the intent you need. 

The website visitor de-anonymization that ZI and Artisan will charge you "credits" for with a 10-20x margin on their API calls. AI workflow with  People Data Labs API calls. Company level website de-anonymization.  Then Job function, seniority, and geo location level persona website de-anonymization. Then API calls for Prospeo.io, Apollo API, Lusha, whatever mix of data providers you need for your territories. All in a customized AI Workflow. 

All integrated with an AI SDR that you have full control over that operates on top of your core systems: CRM (no one replaces one of those.) Sequencing Tool. (We have recommended solutions that actually do that efficiently and have high deliverability. ) 

Who loses? SEO Agencies. Content Agencies. Ads Agencies. SDR Agencies. Replaced. AI workers enhance your in house team and each worker costs less than a quarter of agency fees. 

Our LinkedIn Ads Analysis, Bid Adjustments, Creative/Copywriting AI Worker for example. $20K flat rate. Scale it to your hearts liking. Give it to your entire demand gen team. 

Our SEO/AIO/GEO Worker. Eliminates your content and SEO agency. 100x the output. Results guaranteed. Eliminates the need for moz, semrush, ahrefs, conductor...etc. Your content team of 3 functions like a team of 100. They become power users inside EverWorker overtime then configure the solution we implemented for 20K to extend it themselves even more overtime and make it even more robust. (Still 20K. We want you to innovate.) 

AI Consultancies: Look if you want to light your money on fire. I live in vegas and can point you to the nearest slot machine. (That would be inside Albertsons since I'm currently at home.) If you want to know what AI you shuold implement immediately, 90 days from now, then 2 quarters from now and want to be told that in the first call for free. You talk to us, because we've done it, and we're accountable to getting you results. 

Anyhow. This is the type of disruption that we're living in. And EverWorker isn't the only company driving it. And we're not the only ones telling this story either. (Just ask Gartner)

Don't let AI walk into a shit show

Most of the companies I work with are well-run. Good teams. Real processes. Customers who've stuck around for a decade.

But when it comes to AI, they're trying to hire a senior employee into a house that isn't ready for one.

Here's the reframe most leaders haven't made: AI is not a product. It's an employee.

You're not buying software. You're hiring intelligence. And nobody hires well before doing the hiring work.

Right now, somewhere in your company, an employee just figured out how to do a four-hour task in thirty minutes. They're on salary. They hit their numbers. They're not going to tell you. Why would they?

Right now, another one just pasted a client contract into ChatGPT to summarize it. Didn't mean to leak anything. The data is already gone. You will not find out until something breaks.

Right now, your best senior operator — the one whose knowledge you most need to build anything real — is watching you signal an AI initiative and doing the math on what it means for them. You are asking them to help document themselves out of the room. They are not going to say no. They are going to wait you out.

Three different people. Three rational responses to the incentive structure you have in place today. None of them solvable by a town hall or a Slack memo from the CEO.

This is what it looks like when AI meets a house that isn't ready.
Here's the part nobody tells leaders: you can get enormous ROI from AI right now without hiring one. The tools your people already have, used properly, on workflows you've actually mapped, with incentives that reward disclosure instead of hiding. That's real money sitting on the table.
A saying I keep coming back to: the early bird gets the worm, but the second mouse gets the cheese.

Don't live in FOMO.

Get your shit together first. Then let AI walk in.

Something shifted in the AI conversation over the last few weeks. As people have become quite familiar with AI, they are paying more attention to how leading labs are discussing the future.

People are tired of being told they should be excited about the automation of their own work. The daily user sees real productivity gains. They also see the hiring freeze, the entry-level roles disappearing, and the stagnation in raises.

Then Semafor World Economy happened last week alongside IMF and World Bank meetings. The elite consensus on AI and labor was that there is no elite consensus. The people building this technology and the people governing it cannot agree on the scope of the problem, let alone the response.

However, polling shows a different story where AI polls extremely unfavorably and the future of work is quite murky.

That gap is the story. Two realities running in parallel. One of them will eventually win.

If AI really is as transformative as the hype suggests, the social contract has to be reexamined. That means bringing labor and capital to the same table while there is still time to negotiate. The alternative is waiting until the pushback writes the contract without them.

I wrote about what that delay costs, and why the algorithmic layer is making it harder to organize a response.

LinkedIn is getting rough. For those doing outreach and sales (no shade, I respect founders taking initiative), attached is an example of what works on me, and here are are examples of what doesn't:

Example 1: We’re either vibe coding or helping founders turn MVPs into production-grade products in weeks. How’s your build coming along, are you vibe coding?

Example 2: scoring for AI features & agents. Would love to ask a bit about what you guys are working on & swap notes on evaluation.

Example 3: out of curiosity how are you currently bridging the gap between AI/ML initiatives and hiring the right talent to execute them?

...etc., you get the idea. People have 30+ of these a day in their inboxes. I make it a point to read all of them because some of the best mutual win-win connections I've had over the years are from my LI inbox. Here's what works (for me personally, when someone does outbound):

A positive and outgoing message without an exploitative agenda PLUS something you think they will find interesting and useful, right there in the initial message. Not a link to your article, not a question, not asking if someone wants your mailing list. Don't cognitively burden someone before you offer them value. It's rude.

I do outreach too sometimes, to individuals I think are cool or want to talk with (ML R&D scientists, anyone in a leading R&D lab, etc.). I have autoscans to find people who may be a good fit. If found, I (1) run an LM workflow to give me a condensed bio of that person, (2) run an LM workflow to get the top few relevant links / sources that I think are of interest to them, (3) read the links to see if they genuinely interest me, or if there's recent news I find cool and interesting about those topics, (4) write the message myself if I find such news. No LMs or other sh*tty "mass gamification" tools. I write a genuine message that comes from ME. If I have nothing to offer that person upfront, I don't write a message.

Over the years, my outreach results are 80%+ uptake on the connection and a good convo. One where I don't try to sell sh*t. Most of us are sick of others blatantly acting in their own self-interest at our expense, especially when that's our first impression of them (halo effect, confirmation bias, etc.) and we can see it from a mile away. Especially if they are influential.

You're probably thinking "okay cool, but how do I actually get business for my company?". We do inbound or referrals only. Our minimum contract is a $250K+ annual commitment with 50% upfront and friendly early termination terms (pro-rated refund, only 10 calendar days' notice). We've had 2 terminations since 2023.

It's a small big world, and your reputation is EVERYTHING. We're booked for 1+ years out because that is how we operate and everyone knows that. We do not have capacity to take on more customers because we put them first. If we don't have the resources to give them their money's worth and then some, we don't take the contract.

🧠 AI/ML Engineer — LLMs & Agentic AI
Location: Remote (US) | Dallas, TX / Phoenix, AZ
Type: Contract 
Experience: 3–5 Years
Industry: Technology / AI Consulting
 
About the Role
We're hiring a hands-on AI/ML Engineer to design, build, and deploy production-grade AI solutions for US-based enterprise clients. This is an execution-first role you'll contribute real, working code and be responsible for measurable outcomes, not just prototypes.
 
Key Responsibilities
Design and deploy end-to-end AI/ML solutions from concept to production
Build agentic AI workflows using LangChain, LangGraph, and MCP (Model Context Protocol) with multi-step orchestration and human-in-the-loop controls
Implement RAG pipelines with vector databases (FAISS, Pinecone, MongoDB Atlas) grounded in approved knowledge sources
Develop scalable REST APIs using FastAPI or Flask to expose AI capabilities to downstream services
Integrate AI solutions into CI/CD pipelines (Jenkins, GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps)
Deploy and monitor AI workloads on AWS (SageMaker, Bedrock, Lambda) or GCP (Vertex AI, BigQuery)
Collaborate with Product, Platform, and DevOps teams to ensure secure, scalable, and responsible AI delivery
 
Mandatory Skills
Python — Production-grade development (APIs, pipelines — beyond Jupyter notebooks)
LLMs / GenAI — GPT-4, Claude (Bedrock), Gemini, or open-source (LLaMA, Mistral)
LangChain / LangGraph — Agentic orchestration, tool-use, multi-agent workflows
RAG Architecture — Embeddings, chunking strategies, retrieval grounding
ML Frameworks — PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, or Hugging Face
Cloud — AWS or GCP (deployment, managed AI services)
SQL + Data — Query optimization, JSON/XML/CSV transformations
 
Good to Have
MCP (Model Context Protocol) and model routing frameworks
Docker / Kubernetes for AI workload packaging
Kafka for real-time, event-driven AI pipelines
GitHub Copilot / Cursor.AI for AI-assisted development
Terraform or IaC tools for cloud infra automation
Java (Spring Boot integration with ML models)
 
Qualifications
Bachelor's in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience
3+ years building production-ready AI/ML applications (not just PoCs)
Strong engineering fundamentals — OOP, data structures, debugging, secure coding
Experience in Agile / POD team environments

The Augmented Human and Instant Meritocracy

Room 402 is vacant. The independent professional—recruited by Gemini HR just minutes ago to cover a staff shortage—arrives at the door. In her hands, she holds the smart glasses and earpiece handed to her at the entrance. Her instinct tells her to fear surveillance.

She takes a deep breath and puts them on for the first time. And the lie crumbles.

🛡️ 1. Zero-Trust Security & Biometric Check-in: Hiring in minutes demands absolute compliance. Before she arrives, Gemini HR has instantly cross-referenced credentials and legal backgrounds. As she puts on the smart glasses, a seamless biometric scan ensures the person executing the job is the exact verified professional. No identity fraud, 100% legal compliance.

✨ 2. Augmentation, not Surveillance: There is no pressure, only support. Without knowing the hotel, she becomes an Augmented Professional. Powered by lightning-fast inference infrastructure, the lens guides her to ensure the luxury standard of that specific brand with zero latency, hesitation, or errors.

🥇 3. Visually Validated Pride: She executes the daily cleaning with guided perfection. No guesswork, nothing "swept under the rug." Her human intuition merges with the precision of the machine.

💧 4. Micro-Inventory & Zero Cross-Contamination: As she works, the system visually calculates the exact chemical usage: 25ml of floor detergent, 5ml of glass cleaner, and 5ml of toilet disinfectant. Simultaneously, it tracks the strict usage of microfiber cloths—ensuring the cloth used for the toilet is immediately bagged for laundry and never touches another surface. The stock updates in real-time. Zero manual counting, zero health risks.

💸 5. Visual Proof of Work & Millisecond Meritocracy: The cleaning ends. The visual validation acts as an irrefutable Proof of Work, instantly triggering an unbreakable smart contract. Behind the scenes, a swarm of AI agents orchestrates the workflow, securely routing her day's wage plus an Excellence Bonus to her digital wallet. Zero wage disputes, zero fraud, no bureaucracy.

The true Human Resources Revolution is using Gemini HR to recruit by the minute, guide with precision, and reward those who work best, at the exact moment they deserve it. This is the true power of Agentic Computing and the new era of low-latency AI: Gemini HR doesn't control. Gemini HR rewards.

Would you feel more empowered or intimidated wearing these glasses on your first day? Let me know your thoughts in the comments! 👇

Most AI investments fail in the first 90 days.

Not because the tools don't work.

Because there's no structure for how they operate.

Inside the Taylord AI OS™, AI doesn't function as a tool.

It functions as roles.

11 C-Suite AI Roles — each trained on your institutional intelligence..

The Strategic Layer
CEO — strategic priorities and decision alignment
Chief of Staff — coordination and leadership rhythm
CSO — competitive intelligence and market positioning

The Growth Layer
CPO — offer development and product-market fit
CMO — content, campaigns, and brand consistency
CRO — sales enablement, proposals, and pipeline

The Operational Layer
COO — process documentation and workflow optimization
CTO — tech stack, integrations, and systems architecture
CFO — financial dashboards, pricing, and forecasting

The People & Protection Layer
CHRO — hiring, onboarding, and culture
CLO — compliance, contracts, and risk

Most businesses don't need all 11 on day one.

The audit determines which 3–5 roles create the most leverage for where you are right now.

But here's the principle...

Each role has a defined scope.

Each role operates within governance.

Each role reads from the same context library.

That's why it works.

Without this structure, AI becomes another tool you babysit instead of leverage.

A role with scope, rules, and context isn't a chatbot.

It's infrastructure.

And infrastructure compounds.

AI hiring tools can be the ultimate enemy of the “black sheep” applicant.

If your career path is non-linear: career breaks, contract work, caregiving, disability-related gaps, industry switches, “weird” job titles: an AI screener may read that as “risk” instead of “range.” And keyword-heavy filters can quietly erase great candidates before a human ever sees the story.

My take (as a proud black sheep/unicorn): AI can support hiring, but it can’t be the hiring.

If you want a process that’s more defensible + more human, try this:
1) Audit your knockout criteria (are you filtering for “must-have” or “nice-to-have”?)
2) Add a human review lane for non-traditional profiles
3) Use skills-based evidence (work samples, structured scorecards)
4) Make room for narrative (short prompts like: “What’s the thread in your path?”)

Where are you seeing AI screen-out the very candidates you most want to include?

Sources:
NYC Local Law 144 (Automated Employment Decision Tools): https://lnkd.in/gsaN-UVq
EEOC (Artificial Intelligence and Algorithmic Fairness): https://www.eeoc.gov/ai

The Contract-to-Hire Shift Is Rewriting IT Hiring🔄

Something significant is happening in how US companies are bringing on IT talent, and it's changing the rules of the game for employers and staffing firms alike.📋
Here's what's standing out 👇

💼Contract-to-Hire Is Now the Default for Senior IT Roles: Direct hire is losing ground every quarter. Employers are opting for contract-to-hire arrangements, especially for senior positions, as a way to validate fit before making a long-term commitment.
⚡Speed Is a Dealbreaker: Over 60% of candidates drop off if they wait more than two weeks for a response. In IT, top candidates are off the market fast, firms that can onboard contractors within 48 hours are winning the talent race.
📊The IT Staffing Market Is Growing: The global IT staffing market is valued at approximately $91.6 billion in 2026, with contract-to-hire models representing a growing share of placements as companies prioritize flexibility over headcount.
🧠Uncertainty Is Driving Flexibility: With AI disruption reshaping job functions and economic signals still mixed, 70% of talent now expects hybrid or flexible engagement models as a baseline, not a perk. Clients want agility; talent wants options.
🏛️Public Sector Is a Growth Pocket: Government, healthcare, and infrastructure clients are actively expanding technical headcount in 2026, and specialized staffing partners who know these environments are in high demand.

At Triune, we have been placing IT professionals across public agencies and enterprises for over 21 years. In a market where the hiring model itself is changing, having a staffing partner who knows how to move fast and place right makes all the difference.🔥

Technical Qualifications
● Deep Telco Stack Knowledge: Hands-on experience with 5G SA Core, Network Slicing, and Service Management and Orchestration (SMO) architectures.

"We already use AI."
No, you don't. You just have a ChatGPT tab open.

Most agencies I talk to have "adopted" AI.
They use it for copy.
They use it for briefs.
And yet, their team is still drowning in manual labor.

Here is the invisible leak in your agency:
Someone is still logging into Meta, Google, and LinkedIn one by one.
Someone is still copying numbers into a spreadsheet.
Someone is still pasting that data into ChatGPT, begging it to "make sense of this."
Someone is still formatting that output into a client deck.

You didn't build a system. You just added a tool to a broken process.

In 2026, the gap is widening:

The "AI-Using" Agency:
→ Manual data entry (just faster typing).
→ New client = More hiring.
→ Margins stay thin.

The "AI-Native" Agency:
→ Data pulls, analysis, and reporting happen while the team sleeps.
→ Onboarding triggers the moment the contract is signed.
→ New client = Pure profit.

Same tools. Completely different business models.
One scales with people. The other scales with systems.

AI won't save your agency. Rebuilding around it will.

Happy Automating.

Battery Management Systems and EV Safety:

Battery Management Systems (BMS) act as the brains of electric vehicle batteries. They monitor voltage, current, temperature, and charge level to ensure the battery operates safely and efficiently.

As more people use electric vehicles, Intelligent Battery Management Systems (iBMS) are becoming even more important. An iBMS does more than just basic monitoring. It can spot problems early, improve temperature control, help batteries last longer, and lower the risk of overcharging, overheating, or thermal runaway.

Safety is essential in electric vehicles. A reliable BMS is one of the key systems that protects the battery, passengers, and the vehicle’s performance.

The way data engineers connect LLMs to enterprise data just changed forever.

Databricks MCP Servers just hit Public Preview and if you're not paying attention, you're already behind.

Here's what this actually means👇

MCP (Model Context Protocol) is the new standard for letting AI agents "talk" to your data tools and systems securely, at scale, without you hardcoding every single API connection.

Until now, wiring an LLM to your lakehouse, pipelines, or governance layer was a custom engineering nightmare.
Every project meant rebuilding the bridge from scratch.

With Managed MCP Servers, you expose your data tools as standardized, governed endpoints.
Your AI agent doesn't need to know your internal architecture.
It just calls the tool. Unity Catalog handles the auth, access, and audit trail.

I've spent years building pipelines where the hardest part wasn't the data it was trust.
Trust that the right people accessed the right data. Trust that pipelines ran clean.
MCP bakes that trust directly into how agents interact with your data estate.

This is what agentic data engineering actually looks like in 2026 not just AI writing SQL, but AI operating governed pipelines end to end.

The data engineer's role isn't shrinking. It's becoming the architect of how agents behave.

The teams who figure out MCP governance early will have a serious competitive edge.
The teams who ignore it will be rebuilding their AI integrations in 18 months.

This resume got roles at Google, Meta, and more 🤯

Note: Shared with permission. Info blurred for privacy. (And if you want free resources to level up your own resume, click here: https://bit.ly/4bKLp5p)

Let me break down exactly why it worked and what I'd change. Here's what got them in the door:

💡 Brand stacking that builds on itself: Google -> Meta -> Pure Storage isn't random as each move signals upward trajectory and cross-industry credibility. They used their experience in tech space to lean into more interviews and opportunities at adjacent companies, exemplified in the resume. Sure, having "Ex-(COMPANY)" may sound "weird", but it does bring credibility, let's be honest. People hiring use past company names as a trust shortcut, and this resume earns it fast.

💡 Every bullet has a number attached to it: 56% YoY increase. 167% increase in offer accepts. 600+ leads pipeline. 300+ applications screened per week. This shows how much impact the person had in their role.

💡 Deep DEI specialization across different company types: Big Tech (Google, Meta) to contract work to event execution. This person is a specialist, and companies want specialists nowadays rather than generalists (especially AI being where it's at). They get hired faster because they solve a specific problem with mastery.

Now, what would I change?
- This resume is 2 pages (that's why there's a gap at 3/4 of the screenshot). You don't need two pages unless it's all relevant experience. This resume could easily be 1 -> rule of thumb IMO is 1 page for every 10+ YOE
- Several bullets are 3–4 sentences long. 1-2 sharp line > one long paragraph every time (simpler and more concise)
- The formatting is dense. When a resume doesn't breathe, recruiters skim past it - even great experience gets missed

Bottom line: deep expertise + consistent metrics + recognizable company names = a resume that gets read for more than the average of "6-7 seconds".

Let me know if you all want to see more of this series, and DM me your resume to get a chance to be featured!

[A long post; but stay with me]
last year i wrote about not FIRING anyone when revenue dipped.
this month we shipped automations inside #OneSuite that do what a junior used to do all week.

still figuring out how i feel about it tbh.

in the past,
when things got tight we kept the team.
paid salaries from our pocket.
tightened everywhere else.
i'd do it again.
(i think)

but reality is-
the "junior" layer of EVERY #agency is being quietly rewritten.

chasing signatures.
invoice followups.
copy-pasting lead data into the CRM.
sending status updates.
rebuilding the same proposal for the 40th time.
work i used to hire for
work i used to protect...
Now I have Ai and OneSuite to handle ALL of it.

CRM, projects, invoicing, contracts, payments, client portal. 
it takes me one login. 

The automation doing all grunt work so people dont have to.


so which version of me is right?
the one who paid salaries to keep the team?
or the one building the tool that makes those roles disappear?
idk.
maybe both.

both started from the same place.
and Same beliefs : people arent line items.

look you still had tools in 2015
and you dont hire someone to do work a tool can do.
you hire them for the work it cant.

the judgement calls.
the messy client conversations.
the creative swings.
the decisions that actaully need a human in the room.
let the automations chase the invoice.

let your people do the THINKING.

the founders who treat people as line items will still #LOSE their best talent.
just to AI this time.
not to competitors.

i keep asking myself what id do if revenue dipped today.
honestly?
Situation will say that; 

but id rather have 5 people doing brilliant work.
than 15 drowning in admin.

what are you actually hiring for?

We are now opening a dedicated offline channel focused entirely on the healthcare vertical, and we need someone with the right relationships and the sales instincts to drive it. Your job is to generate qualified conversations with healthcare marketing and patient acquisition leaders who control digital budgets at their organizations. Every introduction you make, every qualified conversation you initiate, every warm handoff you deliver to our sales team is the entire job. We take it from there. Healthcare clients in this space typically sign twelve month contracts and maintain those relationships well beyond that. You open the door once. The relationship sustains itself for years. Your commission is front loaded and paid at the point of conversion. You keep what you bring in and you are immediately back to hunting the next opportunity. Finance leaders: the workforce is evolving. Are you keeping up? Robert Half Executive Director Steve Saah covers the trends that matter most - here were some of the takeaways:

-Hiring remains active, but more selective. Finance leaders are still adding both permanent and contract talent in 2026, yet open roles are taking longer to fill and bad hires are seen as more costly than ever.
-Talent shortages are shifting hiring priorities. With experienced professionals retiring and skilled candidate pools tight, employers are focusing less on volume and more on candidates with adaptability, learning ability, critical thinking, and strong communication skills.
-Stronger vetting is becoming essential. AI-assisted resumes and fake candidate profiles are creating new hiring risks, so finance leaders are increasing emphasis on technical assessments, open-ended interviews, and experience verification.
-Retention and flexibility matter as much as recruiting. Companies are using blended staffing models (permanent + contract talent), improving compensation/flexibility, and increasing manager check-ins to reduce turnover and maintain productivity during peak workloads. https://bit.ly/3OhQevy

Stakeholder Management & Commercial Growth: You will proactively build, nurture, and manage strong, collaborative relationships with key internal stakeholders across various departments, including Client Leadership, Strategy, and Growth teams. The objective is to identify emerging commercial opportunities, translate client needs into viable research projects, and actively contribute to the development and execution of strategic growth plans for the organization. AI-Driven Research Innovation: You will take a leading role in the exploration, development, and practical implementation of cutting-edge AI-driven research methodologies. This involves close collaboration with internal expert teams

7 Simple Steps to Hire AI Developers 

Hiring the right AI talent isn’t just about resumes, it’s about aligning expertise with business outcomes. Here’s a practical, experience-driven framework to hire AI developers effectively:

• Define Clear AI Goals: Set a precise vision, use cases, expected outcomes, timelines, and budget. Avoid vague scopes.
• Identify Required Skills: Look for strong foundations in Python, ML frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch), data engineering, and cloud platforms.
• Choose the Right Hiring Model: Decide between full-time, contract-based, or AI developers for hire via staff augmentation for flexibility.
• Source Talent Strategically: Use LinkedIn, niche AI platforms, and tech communities. Quality sourcing saves time later.
• Conduct Technical Screening: Evaluate real-world problem-solving, not just theoretical knowledge. Include coding and AI use-case tests.
• Validate Experience & Soft Skills: Check past projects, references, and communication skills, critical for long-term success.
• Secure & Onboard Effectively: Finalize contracts with IP protection and define clear milestones from day one.

💡 Expert Insight: Companies that follow a structured hiring approach reduce AI project failure rates significantly and accelerate ROI.

We sat down with Crystel Robbins Rynne, CEO of HRLocker, and the conversation was a wake-up call for any Irish business owner.

Did you know that 2 in 3 Irish SMEs could be at risk of GDPR breaches, simply because of how they store HR records?

Crystel shared a simple but brilliant test for knowing whether your people admin is actually working:

"Can you get the information you're asked for in 15 minutes? If the WRC walks in tomorrow and asks for all of your records, how fast can you get them?"

I've been applying to blockchain related organizations like Anchorage Digital KYC operations, CertiK for their Blockchain Security Expert AI Track & the  Security Audit Track and Chainalysis, Coinbase, & other companies.

Here's what I want to say out loud: I'm positive I'm not the candidate that checks every box.

My Solidity & coding in general is novice. I don't have a Code4rena leaderboard ranking. I haven't shipped a production security agent (still building).

What I do have:
A career spanning enterprise cybersecurity strategy at Xerox where I worked with a very small team to build & manage their cyber operations for majority of the state agencies, secure military video teleconferencing where my reaction time was tested on a daily basis with on the spot management & remediations, and U.S. Army discipline & standards to manage soldiers & equipment across 3 continents including North America 🌎 , Asia 🌏 , & Europe 🌍 . I also independently consult in AI / blockchain / security intersection to hopefully guide unaware people on new technology.

A Master's in Cybersecurity Operations, CompTIA Security+, & a Chainalysis Reactor Certification for blockchain forensics and on-chain intelligence

Active, hands-on work with agentic coding tools & human-in-the-loop AI agent design, which may be the exact skill set this role is built around

A learning roadmap I'm executing in public include smart contract exploit patterns, analytics, & auditing. I'm also teaching myself Blockchain development, continuing my experience with Solidity & learn more about vulnerabilities & tools to include Foundry, Slither, & Cyfrin.  

The job descriptions I read are like a map of the problem space I already sketch on notepads. How do you get an LLM to reason adversarially about smart contract state? How do you translate academic research on agent architectures into something production-grade? How do you keep a human meaningfully in the loop when the agent is surfacing findings faster than any reviewer could?

I may not be the candidate with the deepest audit resume. But I am the candidate actively closing the gaps in public, on purpose. 

How I see it, working in general will at some point be scarce, so being a polymath strategist, which in my case basically mean i've gained knowledge in multiple disciplines which has oddly intersected but wasn't planned: AI/Blockchain/Cyber & my natural ability to communicate adaptively with people so they understand is the best route to thrive in this evolving society. 

"A jack of all trades is a master of none, but oftentimes better than a master of one." Something my professional boxing🥊, landscaping, truck driving🚚 father taught me. Who knew the whole world would need to touch up on being educated enough in KEY topics to produce COMPLIANT & SECURE minded products.  But as he would say, ANYWAY...

If you're building, hiring, discussing at the intersection of AI and blockchain security, I'd love to talk.

Hello Bench Sales Recruiters
Hope you all are doing well
we have a requirement which is based on C2C. Below is mentioned
requirement, revert with your updated resumes to
Email I.D:Harsh@squarehiring.com

Title: Network API Specialist
 Location : Preferably - Southlake , TX 
(2 days in a week in office) /Remote
Duration: Contract

Job description:

We are looking for a visionary Network API Specialist to architect the next generation of our programmable network strategy. You won't just be ""exposing APIs""—you will be designing the economic and technical interface between our core network assets and the burgeoning AI Agent Economy.
Your goal is to move us beyond the baseline standardization of Aduna and GSMA Open Gateway into high-margin, intent-based network services across terrestrial and non-terrestrial domains.

Key Responsibilities
● Vendor Orchestration: Act as the primary technical liaison with Ericsson (Aduna/Vonage) and Nokia (Network as Code). Evaluate their internal roadmaps to ensure our internal capabilities remain ahead of generic ""off-the-shelf"" vendor offerings.
● Examples of Vision Execution:
1. Agentic Interfacing: Architect API wrappers that support Model Context Protocol (MCP) and Intent-Based Networking, allowing AI agents (Gemini, OpenAI, Claude) to negotiate network resources autonomously.
2. Vertical Partnership Development: Identify and build deep-tech API integrations for specific sectors (e.g., Automotive V2X, Remote Healthcare, or Logistics).
3. Multi-Domain Orchestration: Integrate terrestrial 5G-Advanced capabilities with NTN (Satellite) APIs for seamless, transport-agnostic global connectivity.

● Internal Capability Mapping: Audit our current 5G Standalone (5G SA) and IMS cores to identify ""hidden"" data assets that can be productized via APIs.

Technical Qualifications
● Deep Telco Stack Knowledge: Hands-on experience with 5G SA Core, Network Slicing, and Service Management and Orchestration (SMO) architectures.
● API Ecosystem Expert: Proven track record with the CAMARA API framework and the implementation of Network APIs.
● AI/Software Native: Deep understanding of LLM orchestration, Agentic AI workflows, and how software developers (not just telco engineers) consume services.
● Multi-Vendor Experience: Ability to navigate the specific API ""flavors"" of Ericsson/Vonage, Nokia, and AWS/Azure Telco Clouds.

Strategic Competencies
● Commercial Ingenuity: Designs ""Outcome-Based"" solutions that integrate capabilities across domains, business units, and potentially external enterprises.
● Ecosystem Gravity: Possesses a strong industry network to foster collaboration and attract best practices and innovative ideas for the telecommunications sector.
● Wit & Communication: Articulates the distinction between a ""Control Plane"" and a ""Business Outcome"" to an executive audience in under two minutes

Thanks,
Harsh dhiman
Square Hiring LLC • Mesa, Arizona
📱 602-848-1515 (118)
✉️ Harsh@squarehiring.com
